# Loop status — auto-generated; do not edit

**Last fire:** never
**State:** NEVER_RUN

---

This file is auto-regenerated by `/next-task` on every loop fire. Do not edit it manually — your edits will be overwritten on the next fire.

## How to use this file

- **Glance at `State:` at the top.** If `RUNNING` or `ALL_DONE`, no action needed. If any `*FAILED` or `*HALT`, take action per the "What needs your attention" section.
- **Open `BLOCKERS.md`** for full detail on each blocker.
- **Run `/status` slash command** for a fresh snapshot without waiting for the next fire.

## States this file may report

| State | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `NEVER_RUN` | Loop hasn't fired yet | Run `/next-task` once manually to validate, then `/loop 45m /next-task` |
| `RUNNING` | Loop is healthy; tasks committed since last fire | None — let it run |
| `ALL_DONE` | No `[ ]` tasks remain; all done or skipped | Project complete; consider `/loop-stop` |
| `A-RULE-HALT` | Two consecutive `[?]` tasks → halt | Read BLOCKERS.md; resolve at least one `[?]` (edit marker back to `[ ]` in TASKS.md after fixing root cause) |
| `C-RULE-HALT` | Current milestone has `[?]` → halt | Resolve all `[?]`/`[GATE FAILED]` markers in the current milestone |
| `PUSH-CONFLICT` | Push rejected after rebase retry | Manually rebase and resolve; the loop will resume next fire |
| `MILESTONE-GATE-FAILED` | Full build/test gate failed at milestone boundary | Investigate (likely `git bisect` since previous green gate) |
| `CI-FAILED` | origin/main CI is red | Fix CI; the loop will resume next fire |
| `BOOT-CHECK-FAILED` | Pre-flight failed (dirty tree, branch divergence, missing files, etc.) | Fix the listed check; loop resumes on next fire |
| `BLOCKED-NO-ELIGIBLE` | `[?]`/`[!]` exist but no `[ ]` task is eligible (dependency chain) | Resolve blockers in dependency order |
